---
title: "Open Research, Data Stories and Useful Tools"
description: "Matteo's Lab documents open research, data visualizations, software experiments and tools designed to create useful outcomes for people, communities and businesses."
author: "Matteo Arellano"
lang: en
keywords:
  - data research
  - data visualization
  - open research
  - public-interest technology
  - analytics
  - software experiments
  - Matteo Arellano
image: "images/home/matteo-arellano-navigating.jpeg"
image-alt: "Matteo Arellano standing on a boat with mountains and the sea behind him."
page-layout: full
toc: false
title-block-style: none
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Project status

**Researching**

I am currently exploring the available datasets, documenting early hypotheses and testing possible directions for the final project.
:::

## About the project

This is my first data competition.

Over the coming weeks, I will document how I explore the datasets, develop research questions, test hypotheses, create visual experiments and decide what story is worth telling.

The purpose is not only to create a final visualization. I want to understand how data can help communities, support better public decisions and bring attention to issues affecting Pacific and Indigenous populations.

## Current phase

I am beginning with dataset exploration.

My first goals are to understand:

- What information is available
- What each row and variable represents
- Which geographic areas and years are covered
- What information is missing
- Which datasets could be connected
- What meaningful questions the data could answer

## What will be documented

This project will include:

- Research logs
- Hypotheses
- Data-cleaning decisions
- Code and notebooks
- Exploratory visualizations
- Findings and limitations
- Videos
- The final competition submission
- A retrospective after completion

## Current research question

> What important story about Pacific communities can these datasets genuinely support?

This question will evolve as I learn more about the available evidence.

## Explore the project

- [Research Log](log/index.qmd) — Dated notes, experiments and changes in direction
- [Hypotheses](hypotheses.qmd) — Questions and ideas being tested
- [Decision Log](decisions.qmd) — Important analytical and technical choices
